listening_question.text: What's the problem about deciding on a holiday?
listening_question.translation: 决定如何度假有什么为难的地方?
listening_question.answer: Gary and his wife cannot make up their minds about where to go for their holiday, what mode of transport to use, and who will look after their home and pets.

lines.speaker: MARTIN
lines.text: Where are you going to spend your holidays this year, Gary?
lines.translation: 加里，今年你们打算去哪里度假?

lines.speaker: GARY
lines.text: My wife wants to go to Egypt.

lines.speaker: GARY
lines.text: I'd like to go there, too.

lines.speaker: GARY
lines.text: We can't make up our minds.

lines.speaker: MARTIN
lines.text: Will you travel by sea or by air?
lines.translation: 你们乘船去，还是乘飞机去?

lines.speaker: GARY
lines.text: It may be cheaper, but it takes a long time.
lines.translation: 可能是便宜些，但花的时间长。

lines.speaker: MARTIN
lines.translation: 我肯定你们一定会玩得很痛快。

lines.speaker: GARY
lines.translation: 我们可能哪里也去不成。

lines.speaker: GARY
lines.text: My wife always worries too much.
lines.translation: 我妻子总是担心这担心那的。

lines.speaker: GARY
lines.text: Who's going to look after the dog?

lines.speaker: GARY
lines.text: Who's going to look after the house?

lines.speaker: GARY
lines.text: Who's going to look after the garden?

lines.speaker: GARY
lines.text: We have this problem every year.
lines.translation: 我们每年都碰到这类问题。

lines.speaker: GARY
lines.text: In the end, we stay at home and look after everything!
lines.translation: 末了,我们呆在家里来照看一切。

generated_prompts.question: Where's Harry?
generated_prompts.answer: He may be in his room. I'm not sure.
generated_prompts.translation: 哈里在哪里？他可能在他的房间里。我不确定。

generated_prompts.question: Where will he go?
generated_prompts.answer: He may go to the cinema. I'm not sure.
generated_prompts.translation: 他会去哪里？他可能去看电影。我不确定。

generated_prompts.question: Why is he late?
generated_prompts.answer: He may be busy. I'm not sure.
generated_prompts.translation: 他为什么迟到？他可能很忙。我不确定。

generated_prompts.question: What is he doing?
generated_prompts.answer: He may be reading. I'm not sure.
generated_prompts.translation: 他在做什么？他可能在阅读。我不确定。

generated_prompts.question: Do you think she is Danish? (Swedish)

generated_prompts.question: Why was he late?
generated_prompts.answer: He may have been busy. I'm not sure.
generated_prompts.translation: 他为什么迟到了？他可能一直很忙。我不确定。

generated_prompts.question: What was he doing?
generated_prompts.answer: He may have been reading. I'm not sure.
generated_prompts.translation: 他当时在做什么？他可能一直在阅读。我不确定。

generated_prompts.question: Do you think she was Danish? (Swedish)
generated_prompts.answer: I'm not sure. She may have been Swedish.
generated_prompts.translation: 你认为她当时是丹麦人吗？(瑞典人) 我不确定。她当时可能是瑞典人。

writing_exercises.instruction: Read the conversation in Lesson 131 again. Then answer these questions.
writing_exercises.translation: 重读第131课的对话,然后回答以下问题:

items.question: Is Martin talking to Gary?
items.answer: Yes, Martin is talking to Gary.

items.question: Where may Gary and his wife go this year?
items.answer: They may go abroad, possibly to Egypt.

items.question: Who wants to go to Egypt?
items.answer: Gary's wife wants to go to Egypt, and Gary would like to go there too.

items.question: How will they travel?
items.answer: They may travel by sea.

items.question: Isn't it cheaper to travel by sea?
items.answer: Yes, it may be cheaper.

items.question: Doesn't it take a long time?
items.answer: Yes, it takes a long time.

items.question: Why might Gary and his wife not go anywhere?
items.answer: Gary's wife worries too much about who will look after the dog, house, and garden.

writing_exercises.instruction: Answer these questions. 模仿例句回答以下问题。

examples.question: Do you think she is Danish? (Swedish)
examples.answer: I'm not sure. She may be Swedish.

examples.question: Do you think she was Danish? (Swedish)
examples.answer: I'm not sure. She may have been Swedish.

items.question: Do you think they are Canadian? (Australian)
items.answer: I'm not sure. They may be Australian.

items.question: Do you think she is Finnish? (Russian)
items.answer: I'm not sure. She may be Russian.

items.question: Do you think they are Japanese? (Chinese)
items.answer: I'm not sure. They may be Chinese.

items.question: Do you think they were butchers? (bakers)
items.answer: I'm not sure. They may have been bakers.

items.question: Do you think she was a dentist? (doctor)
items.answer: I'm not sure. She may have been a doctor.

items.question: Do you think he is a sales rep? (the boss)
items.answer: I'm not sure. He may be the boss.

items.question: Do you think he is seventeen? (twenty-one)
items.answer: I'm not sure. He may be twenty-one.

items.question: Do you think they were five? (seven)
items.answer: I'm not sure. They may have been seven.

items.question: Do you think he was seventy-six? (over eighty)
items.answer: I'm not sure. He may have been over eighty.

items.question: Do you think she was fifty-five? (under fifty)
items.answer: I'm not sure. She may have been under fifty.

items.question: Do you think it is the 17th today? (16th)
items.answer: I'm not sure. It may be the 16th today.

items.question: Do you think it was Tuesday yesterday? (Wednesday)
items.answer: I'm not sure. It may have been Wednesday yesterday.

items.question: Do you think it is the 19th today? (20th)
items.answer: I'm not sure. It may be the 20th today.

items.question: Do you think it is cheap? (expensive)
items.answer: I'm not sure. It may be expensive.

items.question: Do you think it was easy? (difficult)
items.answer: I'm not sure. It may have been difficult.

items.question: Do you think she was old? (young)
items.answer: I'm not sure. She may have been young.

items.question: Do you think he was ill? (tired)
items.answer: I'm not sure. He may have been tired.

items.question: Do you think they are listening to the radio? (watching television)
items.answer: I'm not sure. They may be watching television.

items.question: Do you think she was retiring? (looking for a new job)
items.answer: I'm not sure. She may have been looking for a new job.

items.question: Do you think they are sitting? (standing)
items.answer: I'm not sure. They may be standing.

notes_on_text.title: 1. We may go abroad.
notes_on_text.text: In English, 'may' is used to express possibility. It is followed by the base form of the verb. Note that 'may' indicates a possibility, which is different from the inference based on evidence discussed in Lesson 127.
notes_on_text.translation_text: 在英文中用 may 来表示可能发生的事情,在may 后面加动词原形。请注意 may 表示的是一种可能性,这与第127课所讲的表示有根据的推论是有区别的。

notes_on_text.title: 2. make up our minds
notes_on_text.text: This idiom means to decide something.

notes_on_text.title: 3. Don't be so sure.
notes_on_text.text: In imperative sentences, the negative form of 'be' is 'don't be'. This structure is often used for advice or warning.
notes_on_text.translation_text: 在祈使句中,be动词的否定形式要用 don't be,这种结构常常用于劝告。

notes_on_text.title: 4. We might not go anywhere.
notes_on_text.translation: 我们可能哪里也去不成。
notes_on_text.text: 'Might' can also express possibility, but it suggests a lower degree of certainty than 'may'. Like 'may', 'might' can refer to present or future possibility.
notes_on_text.translation_text: might 可以用来表示一种可能性,但它表示的“肯定”程度还不如may。和may一样,might 所表示的是现在或将来的一种可能性。

notes_on_text.title: 5. look after
notes_on_text.text: This phrasal verb means to take care of someone or something.

notes_on_text.title: 6. in the end
notes_on_text.text: This phrase means finally or eventually.

grammar_sections.title: Expressing Possibility with 'May' and 'Might'
grammar_sections.explanation: 'May' and 'might' are modal verbs used to express possibility. 'May' suggests a higher chance of something happening, while 'might' suggests a lower chance. Both can refer to present or future possibilities. For past possibilities, we use 'may/might have + past participle'.

examples.translation: 我们可能哪里也去不成。

examples.text: He may be in his room.

grammar_sections.title: Negative Imperatives with 'Don't be'
grammar_sections.explanation: To form a negative imperative with the verb 'be', we use 'Don't be + adjective/noun'. This is used to advise or warn someone not to be in a certain state or act in a certain way.

segments.title: Setting the Scene: Holiday Dilemmas
segments.description: Introduce the lesson's theme: planning a holiday and the difficulties involved. Ask students about their own holiday planning experiences to engage them. Briefly mention the main characters, Martin and Gary, and the central question of the dialogue.

segments.title: Dialogue Breakdown: Understanding Gary's Holiday Woes
segments.description: Go through the dialogue line by line. Explain new vocabulary and phrases in context (e.g., 'go abroad', 'make up our minds', 'look after'). Pay special attention to expressions of uncertainty and possibility like 'I'm not sure', 'We may go...', 'It may be...', and 'Don't be so sure'. Discuss the cultural aspects of holiday planning and family concerns.

segments.title: Grammar Spotlight: Expressing Possibility with 'May' and 'Might'
segments.description: Focus on the core grammar point: using 'may' and 'might' to express possibility. Explain the difference in certainty between 'may' and 'might'. Introduce 'may/might have + past participle' for past possibilities, using examples from Lesson 132's pattern drills. Practice forming sentences with these structures based on various scenarios.

segments.title: Key Vocabulary: Words for Travel and Uncertainty
segments.description: Review the new words and expressions from Lesson 131 (e.g., 'Egypt', 'abroad', 'worry', 'make up one's mind', 'look after', 'in the end'). Then, introduce key vocabulary from Lesson 132's pattern drills that describe states, locations, and activities (e.g., 'in his room', 'go to the cinema', 'busy', 'reading', 'Danish', 'Canadian', 'butchers', 'dentist', 'cheap', 'ill'). Provide example sentences and encourage students to create their own.

segments.title: Putting It All Together: Practice with Possibilities
segments.description: Engage students in guided practice using the patterns and vocabulary learned. Use the pattern drills from Lesson 132, asking students to answer questions about possibilities in the present and past. Incorporate the written exercises from Lesson 132 Part B, guiding students to form answers using 'I'm not sure. He/She/They may/may have been...'. Encourage pair work and role-playing based on the dialogue.

practice_items.question: 别那么肯定！
practice_items.hint: This is an imperative sentence expressing doubt.

practice_items.question: We may go abroad.
practice_items.hint: Focus on 'may' for possibility and 'abroad' for location.

practice_items.question: He may be busy.
practice_items.hint: Listen carefully to the modal verb and the adjective.

practice_items.question: She may have been reading.
practice_items.hint: Practice the pronunciation of 'may have been' and the '-ing' form.

practice_items.question: Do you think they are Japanese? (Chinese)
practice_items.answer: I'm not sure. They may be Chinese.

practice_items.question: Do you think he was ill? (tired)
practice_items.answer: I'm not sure. He may have been tired.
practice_items.pattern_template: I'm not sure. {subject} may have been {alternative}.
practice_items.hint: Use 'may have been' for past possibility.
